description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a roughly triangular profile and a rugged, natural surface. The stone displays a mix of coarse and smoother textures, with one side appearing more jagged and layered, while the opposite face is flatter and more weathered. Its coloration is predominantly a light beige to tan, with subtle variations and a small, distinct reddish-brown mark on the upper surface. The edges are uneven and chipped, suggesting natural fracturing rather than artificial shaping. The stone's mass and size indicate it could serve as a substantial obstacle or barrier in an urban environment, such as a park or roadside. Its surface is matte and rough, with visible grain and minor pitting, indicative of prolonged exposure to the elements.
description: A large, irregular beige-tan stone with rough, jagged surfaces and a small reddish-brown mark on top. Its natural, weathered appearance makes it suitable as an urban obstacle or barrier.
description_view_0: The stone's top surface is visible, showing a flat area with a reddish-brown mark and pronounced layering along the side.
description_view_1: This view highlights the broad, relatively flat face of the stone, with subtle color variations and a smoother texture.
description_view_2: The stone appears more block-like from this angle, with a prominent, rough edge and less visible surface detail.
description_view_3: A jagged, angular end of the stone is visible, with deep textural grooves and the reddish-brown mark just visible on the upper surface.
attribute_location: urban park boundary or roadside verge
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular natural grain with subtle layering and a small, distinct reddish-brown patch; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with minor pitting and chipped edges, indicating prolonged exposure to outdoor elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or manicured urban surfaces but may blend with natural landscaping or other stones nearby.
